prompt_text: Open source or affordable ATS tools instead of Workable
response:
If you want **open source**, the strongest direct alternatives to Workable in your results are **FreeATS**, **OpenCATS**, and **SpotAxis**. If you want **affordable but not open source**, the most practical low-cost options mentioned are **Loxo’s free ATS**, **SmartRecruiters SmartStart**, **Zoho Recruit’s free plan**, and **Freshteam/Breezy** as low-cost paid alternatives.[1][3][4][6][9]

A quick way to choose:

| Option | Type | Best for | Main tradeoff |
|---|---|---|---|
| **FreeATS** | Open source ATS | Teams wanting a modern open-source option | Limited public detail in the result; check maturity before adopting.[1] |
| **OpenCATS** | Open source ATS | Basic candidate pipeline tracking | Dated UI, no integrations, no mobile, and self-hosting overhead.[2][5][7] |
| **SpotAxis** | Open source ATS | Teams wanting an MIT-licensed open-source ATS | Public result is only a GitHub project snippet, so maturity/support need verification.[3] |
| **Loxo free ATS** | Free/freemium | Small teams wanting a ready-to-use free ATS | Free tier details are marketed on the vendor site, so limits may apply.[6] |
| **SmartRecruiters SmartStart** | Freemium | Companies under 250 employees | Free plan has caps, including up to 10 active job postings.[4] |
| **Zoho Recruit free** | Freemium | Very small recruiting needs | Free version supports only one active job.[4] |

What the results suggest:

- **Open source does not mean zero cost**: you still need hosting, setup, and maintenance; one source estimates a basic VPS at about **$5–$20/month** and setup time of at least a half day for simpler tools.[2]
- **OpenCATS** is the clearest established open-source ATS in the results and is described as a free, open-source candidate/applicant tracking system for recruiters.[5][7]
- **FreeATS** is notable because it offers a **cloud version free for up to 3 open positions forever**, which may be the easiest “open-source-ish” low-cost path if you want something hosted.[1]
- If your bottleneck is **sourcing candidates**, not just tracking them, traditional ATS tools may not solve the core problem; the source notes that open-source ATSs mainly manage candidates you’ve already found.[2]

If you tell me your team size and whether you need **self-hosted**, **cloud-hosted**, or **no-code/easy setup**, I can narrow this to the best 3 choices.
